Verify the correct account name and password. If you know the unlock code, restart the device and sign in with local administrator credentials. If the device is locked remotely, contact the Service Desk immediately so a manager can review the incident and investigate the lock reason. Do not install unknown remote unlocking tools.

Do not open attachments or click on unknown links in email or social media messages. Use the approved antivirus client and scan all removable media. If prompted, enter the business version of your Microsoft account and device name. If you cannot connect immediately, reboot and test one permitted approved network share.

Verify correct account and device name, test another approved network, and restart the app. Repeated failed attempts or large-scale failures require escalated support. Never use unknown open networks. Include the exact error code, time, location, and whether other users are affected.

Call the Service Desk immediately so a security incident response record can be created. Provide the asset tag, serial number, exact time of theft, location, and whether confidential data was accessed. Do not delay reporting or use unofficial hotspots.

Install approved packages from the package manager or a known-good ISO. If the package is missing, submit a service request with the required metadata, version, source, and intended use. Do not install unofficial binaries.

Verify the correct device type, business network, and location. Reconnect using the approved app and two-factor authentication. If prompted, complete identity verification before accepting the connection. Repeated failed attempts require a security incident report.

Call the Service Desk immediately so a security incident response team can assess and contain the exposure. Provide the asset tag, device type, last known good time, and whether unknown users were present. If possible, collect evidence of credential theft and store it in a secure incident-reporting container.

Do not accept unknown USB drives, emails, or unexpected attachments. Report it immediately so removable media can be sanitized and the source identified. If you have administrator privileges, contact the Service Desk so cleanup can avoid spreading malware.

Check battery status and connect to a known-good power source if available. Restart the device, close unnecessary applications, close remote-desktop connections, and check disk space. Reboot the domain controller if possible. Do not disable endpoint protection unless instructed.

Do not share credentials, approve unknown MFA prompts, or click suspicious links. Call the Service Desk with the account name, business reason, time, and location. The approver must verify the request and approve it before the action is allowed.
